q: Transition elements are the metals that exist in the block between Groups ___ and III.
Transition elements are in Groups 3 to ___ of the Periodic Table.
___ is still important as a structural material and used as a catalyst in the manufacture of ammonia.
Iron is also present in ___.
___ is important as an electronic conductor.
Copper-nickel (cupronickel) alloys are used in ___ due to their excellent resistance to seawater corrosion.
___ is the transition element that is used as jewellery because of its beautiful yellow lustre colour and its inertness to oxygen and water.
___ is a major component in modern jet aircraft and is used in manufacturing artificial joints and dental implants.
The role of ___, an essential component of vitamin $B_{12}$ in living systems, is equally important.
A very tiny trace amount of ___ is detected in apples.
The transition elements are located between Group II of the ___ and Group III of the $p$-block elements in the Periodic Table.
IUPAC defines a transition element (metal) as an element whose atom has partially filled ___ (orbitals).
Under this definition, groups 3 to 12 are the transition elements and the $f$-block lanthanide and actinide series are called the ___.
There are mainly three series of transition elements. The first series $3d$ transition elements run from scandium ($_{21}Sc$) through ___ ($_{30}Zn$).
The second series $4d$ transition elements run from ___ ($_{39}Y$) through cadmium ($_{48}Cd$).
The transition elements are also called the ___ because the $d$ orbitals are filled in these elements.
The first row of the transition elements is in the fourth row (Period ___) of the Periodic Table.
In the $3d$ series transition elements, the nuclear charge increases from ___ to copper.
The decrease in atomic radii occurs because the attraction force between the nucleus and electrons is stronger than the ___ force.
All the transition elements in this series have quite similar ___, which accounts for their ability to blend together to form alloys.
Similarly, the first ___ energies (IE) of scandium to copper increase only slightly as the positive charge on the nucleus (atomic number) increases.
Towards the end of the series, ___ has the larger atomic size and ionisation energy.
In atoms of the transition elements, the ___ orbital is normally filled and the rest of the electrons occupy the $3d$ orbitals.
The filling of the $3d$ orbitals generally obeys ___, with one electron added to each of the five $d$ orbitals before a second electron is added to any one of them.
There are two exceptions to the expected regular filling pattern (half-filled or completely filled orbitals); ___ ($_{24}Cr$) and copper ($_{29}Cu$).
In the case of $_{24}Cr$, each of these orbitals must be filled with each electron to attain a more stable electronic configuration with the ___ $3d$ orbitals.
The essential electronic configuration of $_{29}Cu$ is $[Ar] 4s^{1} 3d^{10}$ because completely filled $3d$ orbitals have ___.
In general, the neutral atoms of most of the transition elements have an essential electronic configuration of ___.
When these metals form ions, the electrons are always removed first from the ___ orbital and then from the $3d$ orbitals.
The ___ element has completely filled $3d$ orbitals.
Copper is considered a transition element because the ___ ion has incomplete $3d$ orbitals.
In a period, the properties of transition elements are very similar because the ___ shell electronic configuration of the elements does not change.
These transition elements are solid metals and have typical metallic properties such as ___, ductility, metallic lustre, and high tensile strength.
They are generally good conductors of ___ and heat.
One cubic centimetre ($1 \text{ cm}^{3}$) cube of iron is 8 times heavier than a $1 \text{ cm}^{3}$ cube of ___.
Transition elements are significantly less reactive than ___ metals.
Iron combined with non-metal carbon produces an alloy called ___.
Most transition elements form ___ compounds.
Transition elements show ___ oxidation states, and they can form more than one compound with another element.
Most transition elements can form ___ ions.
Transition elements typically have incompletely filled $d$ orbitals or readily give rise to ions with ___ $d$ orbitals.
Transition elements usually lose the electron from the ___ orbital first, which is followed by the removal of an electron from the $d$ orbitals.
The $+3$ oxidation states (i.e., $M^{3+}$ ions) are more stable at the ___ of the series.
The highest oxidation state is +7 for ___ ($_{25}Mn$).
The ions of chromium, manganese, and iron have the highest oxidation states, ___, +7, and +6, respectively.
These ions tend to be good ___ agents, e.g., dichromate ion ($Cr_{2}O_{7}^{2-}$), permanganate ion ($MnO_{4}^{-}$), and ferrate(VI) ion ($FeO_{4}^{2-}$).
Transition metals usually have their highest oxidation states in compounds with highly ___ elements such as oxygen and fluorine.
The colour of compounds of transition metals is due to the presence of ___ $d$ orbitals or the presence of unpaired electrons.
Transition metal compounds form coloured ions in an aqueous solution because they absorb part of the ___ light and transmit the complement colour.
The characteristic colour of transition metal ions is helpful when performing ___ analysis.
$Sc^{3+}$ and $Zn^{2+}$ ions are colourless because $Sc^{3+}$ ion has completely ___ $d$ orbitals.
If the electrons are unpaired then it is a ___ substance.
If the electrons are paired, then it is a ___ substance.
___ is a paramagnetic substance whereas copper and zinc are diamagnetic substances.
Substances that are attracted very strongly by the applied magnetic field are said to be ___.
Iron, cobalt, and nickel are ___ substances.
Transition metals are widely used at the industrial level due to their ___ properties.
Iron is used as a catalyst in the manufacture of ammonia (___ process).
Vanadium is used in the form of vanadium(V) oxide ($V_{2}O_{5}$) in the manufacture of sulphuric acid (___ process).
___ is used as a catalyst in the hydrogenation of alkenes.
Titanium(IV) chloride ($TiCl_{4}$) is used for the manufacturing of high-density ___ in industries.
Paramagnetic substance is a substance that has ___ and shows weak attraction towards the external magnetic field.
___ substance is a substance that has no unpaired electrons and shows no attraction (slight repulsion) from the external magnetic field.
Complex ion is an ion, which has a ___ at its centre with a number of other molecules or ions surrounding it.
Titanium is an integral part of modern construction because it is highly ___, strong, and lightweight.
Titanium is used for roofs, flooring, and ___ in buildings.
Titanium is also used for spacecraft, ships, and ___ equipment.
Iron is usually too soft to be used as metal alone; it is usually mixed with small amounts of other elements to make harder ___ steels.
Manganese is mainly used in alloys, such as ___.
Steel contains about ___ % manganese to increase the strength and also improve workability and resistance.
Manganese steel is extremely strong and is used for ___ tracks.
Vanadium is used to form alloys of steel with ___.
Vanadium metal sheets, wires and tubes are widely used in the ___ industry.
Vanadium compounds are used as a ___ in the printing and dyeing of fabrics.
Chromium has many industrial uses due to its toughness and resistance to ___ and corrosion.
Various chromium salts are the main raw materials in the ___ industry.
Chromium is involved in the production of ___ pads in the automobile industry.
Manganese is used as a catalyst to reduce the ___ in gasoline.
Manganese is also used in ___ batteries, and paint.
___ ion has brilliant blue colour used as paint, ink, and pigments.
___ batteries are made up of cobalt.
Nickel is used for anti-corrosion coating, and ___.
Nickel is primarily used in the production of ___.
___ is a good conductor of electricity and it is used for electrical wire, circuit boards, water pipes, and industrial machinery.
Iron and copper are essential metals used in ___ medicine.
Iron is used to treat or prevent ___.
Copper has been used to treat ___ disease and Menkes disease.
The surgical instruments are made of transition metals such as stainless steel, ___ alloys, titanium and its alloys.
Titanium has been used mostly for fabricating ___.
Titanium is also used in artificial hip bone replacements, and ___ frames.
The red colour of ruby is due to a trace amount of ___ ions.
The blue colour of sapphire is due to a trace amount of iron and ___.
Copper is used in agriculture as a ___ (Bordeaux mixture).
___ wire is an alloy that comes into existence by mixing chromium and nickel.
Nichrome wire is used in ___ stoves.
Most paints contain the compound of a ___ element.
The coloured compounds of transition elements can be seen in the ___ glazes.
Transition metals such as iron, chromium, copper, manganese, and zinc are essential ___ for human beings and plants.
Transition metal complexes have a long history of use as ___ and antiviral agents.
